explanation: This command simulates a malicious use of rundll32.exe with shell32.dll and Control_RunDLL, targeting a DLL in the %Temp% directory, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses rundll32.exe with shell32.dll and Control_RunDLL to execute a DLL in the %LocalAppData% directory, which aligns with the Sigma rule's focus on suspicious directories.
This command simulates a benign-looking execution of rundll32.exe with shell32.dll and Control_RunDLL, targeting a DLL in the %AppData% directory, which is flagged by the Sigma rule.
This command uses rundll32.exe with shell32.dll and Control_RunDLL to execute a DLL in the Users\Public directory, which is considered a suspicious location by the Sigma rule.
This command simulates a test scenario where rundll32.exe is used with shell32.dll and Control_RunDLL to execute a DLL in the %tmp% directory, matching the Sigma rule's detection logic.
